behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because the failure boundary is the handoff between embedding output and vector-index schema (module boundary), not a single pure function. Pass 2 downgrade check: Unit is too low because it would only test local length-check logic, not the real wiring between embedder and index config. Pass 2 upgrade check: System/Agentic rejected; behavior text does not require real HTTP/OS/subprocess, and it checks dimensionality contract (deterministic), not model-output quality. Rejection gate for higher levels: behavior only states 'correct dimensionality for the vector database index,' not runtime network behavior or semantic quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run embedding adapter + index schema metadata together in-process (test double for transport allowed). Generate an embedding for a fixed query through the adapter used by production code, then assert vector length equals the configured index dimension and that mismatch is surfaced as an error.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, since this behavior is about interaction between search module, score extraction, and ordering logic. Pass 2 downgrade check: Unit could test sorting alone but would miss module handoff bugs (e.g., wrong score field/normalization). Pass 2 upgrade check: System rejected because behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no LLM/AI quality dependence is stated for this step. Integration-brake: no explicit real infra, no real AI-quality dependency, no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use a local test vector store or in-process repository fixture with known vectors and expected cosine scores. Execute the real similarity-search service path and assert returned items are strictly sorted by descending cosine score, including tie-handling expectations.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Agentic, because correctness depends on real LLM judgment for 'nuanced intent.' Pass 2 downgrade check: Integration with stubs would always pass/fail by fixture design and can hide real ranking-quality failures. Pass 2 upgrade check: Workflow rejected because this behavior is scoped to one agentic step (the re-ranker), not multiple agentic stages in sequence as the thing under test. Rejection gate for Workflow: behavior text explicitly says 'LLM re-ranking step' (single step).
behaviors.test_description: For a curated set of nuanced queries and candidate results, run the real LLM re-ranker (no stubbed ranking output). Compare raw similarity order vs re-ranked order and assert that at least one query exhibits a meaningful order change; repeat across runs with tolerance for nondeterminism.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because this is threshold/filter behavior across retrieval and API response shaping. Pass 2 downgrade check: Unit is too low because the bug can occur when score outputs, cutoff config, and response mapper interact. Pass 2 upgrade check: System rejected since no explicit requirement for real network/OS/subprocess; Agentic rejected because behavior text does not require judging semantic quality from real LLM output, only enforcing empty-result policy when scores are not close. Rejection gate for higher levels: behavior states 'returns an empty result set' policy, not model-quality evaluation.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local test dataset intentionally far from an out-of-domain query and run the real search+post-filter path in-process. Assert API layer returns an empty list when all similarities are below the configured minimum-relevance threshold, with no fallback noisy items.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Workflow, because the measured SLO spans multiple sequential model-driven stages (embedding + retrieval + LLM re-rank) in one pipeline. Pass 2 downgrade check: System alone is too low if it isolates components; this behavior is explicitly end-to-end median latency of the full chain. Pass 2 upgrade check: no higher level defined. Integration-brake confirms upgrade: real infra is involved and multiple agentic/model steps are chained.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like end-to-end requests through the real API path with real model endpoints enabled for both embedding and re-ranking, using a representative query set. Record per-request latency from ingress to response, compute median, and fail if median exceeds 2.0s.
